CREATE TABLE ip_addresses (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip inet);
SELECT id FROM ip_addresses WHERE ip <= (SELECT ip FROM ip_addresses WHERE id = 2);
SELECT segment FROM network_segments WHERE segment <= '192.168.1.5';
SELECT a.id FROM ip_addresses AS a JOIN ip_addresses AS b ON a.ip <= b.ip WHERE b.ip = '192.168.1.5';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ip_addresses WHERE ip <= ANY (SELECT ip FROM ip_addresses WHERE ip > '192.168.1.2');
CREATE TABLE filtered_ips AS SELECT ip FROM ip_addresses WHERE ip <= '192.168.1.4';
SELECT b.id FROM ip_addresses AS a JOIN filtered_ips AS b ON a.ip <= b.ip WHERE a.ip = '192.168.1.3';
